target.title: [perf] improve reply send performance
target.body:
## Summary

- Duplicate of #85853, rebased onto current `openclaw/openclaw:main` at `0ba6b235349add2d1d47b6543b980ee90d93a0df`.
- Keeps both commits from the original stack:
  - `fix: avoid blocking reply sends on bookkeeping`
  - `fix telegram progress tool result preview`
- Improves reply latency by avoiding dispatcher-idle waits on every direct block reply callback.
- Flushes pending block delivery only before subsequent tool/progress events, preserving block-before-tool ordering without making lightweight replies wait on dispatcher bookkeeping.
- Schedules source reply transcript mirroring after successful `message.action` sends without blocking the gateway response, while serializing async mirrors per session to preserve transcript chronology.
- Sends Telegram direct-message typing earlier after access checks so delayed context/session construction does not postpone the visible typing cue.

## Regression Source

#83722 is the merged regression source. It correctly fixed same-channel pre-tool block ordering by waiting for block reply delivery before tool execution could continue, but the merged implementation placed that delivery barrier directly on the hot block-reply callback path.

That means lightweight/direct replies can wait for dispatcher idle or delivery bookkeeping even when no later tool/progress event is about to be emitted. Under concurrent embedded runs, cron work, slow channel I/O, or transcript mirroring pressure, the ordering fix from #83722 turns into amplified user-visible reply latency.

This PR keeps the ordering guarantee introduced by #83722, but moves the wait to the point where ordering matters: before a later tool/progress emission is sent. Direct block replies can resolve without waiting on dispatcher idle, while a subsequent event still flushes pending block delivery first.

## Impact

Simple visible replies can return sooner without compromising user-visible send order. ACP direct block replies remain non-blocking for the common path, block-before-tool/progress chronology is preserved, transcript mirroring stays best-effort after successful source sends, and Telegram direct typing begins earlier without adding a duplicate immediate cue.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: direct visible replies should not be delayed by dispatcher idle waits, source transcript mirroring, or late Telegram typing setup while preserving block-before-tool/progress ordering.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on this rebased branch, with `origin/main` fetched to `0ba6b235349add2d1d47b6543b980ee90d93a0df` before PR creation.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git diff --check`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.typing.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`.

Evidence after fix: branch contains exactly the two duplicated commits on top of current `origin/main`; `git diff --check` was clean; focused Vitest passed 7 files / 381 tests.

Observed result after fix: reply dispatch, ACP delivery, gateway send transcript mirroring, Telegram typing, bot message, and Telegram dispatch regression coverage all passed on the rebased stack.

What was not tested: live Telegram/WhatsApp/Discord account roundtrip with real external credentials.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.typing.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`

target.title: fix(webchat): stabilize live transcript run state
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #83528 and #82611; refs #83949.

- Mirror Codex app-server inbound prompts at turn start with the existing `${turnId}:prompt` identity so WebChat can see external-channel user messages before the turn finishes, while preserving `suppressNextUserMessagePersistence` for already-persisted inbound prompts.
- Keep hidden external-channel live runs private by sending chat/tool/agent updates only to exact selected-session message subscribers instead of broadcasting globally.
- Subscribe Control UI to the selected session message stream on connect/reconnect and session switch, adopt observed run ids for non-WebChat-originated runs including the default main alias/canonical key pair, dedupe accumulated stream snapshots around tool cards, and keep selected-session `session.message` reloads alias-aware.
- Emit stable `messageSeq` values for multi-message Codex mirror batches so selected-session subscribers receive per-message transcript positions instead of post-batch duplicates.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: WebChat/Control UI now has durable inbound prompt truth at Codex turn start, selected-session live updates for external-channel runs without unrelated-session leakage, passive run-id adoption and `session.message` handling across the default main alias/canonical key pair, reconnect-safe selected-session subscription state, stable mirrored transcript message sequences, suppression-safe prompt mirroring, and no duplicated accumulated stream text around tool cards.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw Codex worktree on branch `meow/webchat-transcript-run-state-truth`, latest head `e19523ba6c`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -- -t "does not mirror the Codex prompt early when user message persistence is suppressed"`
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/transcript-mirror.test.ts`
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts`
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.sessions.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway-chat-load.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
  - `node --import tsx scripts/check-no-extension-test-core-imports.ts`
  - `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- Evidence after fix:
  - Suppression regression: failed before the guard because the forbidden prompt appeared; passed after the guard.
  - Codex app-server tests: 2 files passed, 233 tests passed.
  - Gateway/session event tests: 4 files passed, 111 tests passed.
  - Control UI tests: 7 files passed, 238 tests passed.
  - Rebased-base test-type command passed.
  - Boundary check passed: `check-no-extension-test-core-imports` checked 1915 extension files and 1 plugin helper.
  - `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD` passed on latest head.
  - The regressions prove message-bearing prompt transcript updates with message ids and stable sequences, suppressed prompt persistence does not early-mirror a duplicate user row, multi-message mirror batch `messageSeq` `[1, 2]`, selected-session sequence delivery, alias/canonical subscription de-dupe, stale subscription completion cleanup, main-alias `session.message` reloads, and passive main-alias run-id adoption.
- Observed result after fix: The early Codex prompt mirror writes one user transcript row and emits a message-bearing selected-session update before assistant completion only when prompt persistence is not suppressed; final mirrored batches emit correct per-message sequence metadata; hidden external-channel chat events route only to exact selected-session subscribers; selected Control UI sessions subscribe/re-subscribe without alias churn or stale async overwrite; canonical `agent:main:main` transcript events reload selected `main`; passive chat state adopts observed run ids; and accumulated stream snapshots render only their new suffix after tool cards.
- What was not tested: Live Feishu or Telegram external-channel browser proof was not run in this pass. Remote `pnpm check:changed` through Crabbox was attempted before this follow-up but blocked: Blacksmith Testbox is not configured for `.github/workflows/crabbox-hydrate.yml`, and default AWS Crabbox could not refresh credentials because no EC2 IMDS role/credentials were available on this machine.

## Verification

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -- -t "does not mirror the Codex prompt early when user message persistence is suppressed"`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/transcript-mirror.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.sessions.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway-chat-load.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `node --import tsx scripts/check-no-extension-test-core-imports.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`

target.title: fix(agents): log warnings instead of swallowing subagent errors
target.body:
## Problem

Two catch blocks in the subagent registry silently discard errors, leaving no diagnostic trail when hook emission or disk restore fails:

1. **`emitSubagentEndedHookOnce`** (`subagent-registry-completion.ts:116`): `catch { return false; }` discards hook-runner errors when emitting the `subagent_ended` lifecycle hook.
2. **`restoreSubagentRunsOnce`** (`subagent-registry.ts:645`): `catch { // ignore restore failures }` discards errors when restoring subagent runs from disk on cold start.

When either path fails (hook runner unavailable, corrupt disk state, reconciliation error), the system continues silently. Operators have no indication that subagent lifecycle hooks were skipped or that restored runs were lost.

## Fix

Wire `log.warn()` into both catch blocks so errors are logged before the existing fallback behavior continues:

1. **Hook emission path**: `catch (err) { log.warn("failed to emit subagent_ended hook for run ${runId}: ..."); return false; }`
2. **Restore path**: `catch (err) { log.warn("failed to restore subagent runs from disk: ..."); }`

Both paths still return/continue as before (no behavior change beyond logging). The completion file gets its own `createSubsystemLogger("agents/subagent-registry-completion")` to match the existing `createSubsystemLogger("agents/subagent-registry")` in the registry file.

Production diff is +8 lines changed across 2 files.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Two silent `catch` blocks in the subagent registry discard errors during hook emission and disk restore. The fix adds `log.warn()` to both paths so failures leave a diagnostic trail instead of being silently swallowed.

Real environment tested: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2), Node.js v22.16.0, OpenClaw built from source (commit 1d5b5db) at `/tmp/fix-82943`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
Step 1. Build OpenClaw from patched source
cd /tmp/fix-82943
CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm build

Step 2. Verify both log.warn calls are in compiled production code
grep -n "failed to emit subagent_ended hook" dist/subagent-registry-DLVHJynj.js
grep -n "failed to restore subagent runs" dist/subagent-registry-DLVHJynj.js

Step 3. Start the patched gateway
timeout 10 node openclaw.mjs gateway

Step 4. Run test suites for both changed files
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry-completion.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix: terminal output copied below.

**Compiled production code verification**

Both `log.warn` calls are present in the compiled subagent-registry bundle:

```console
$ grep -n "failed to emit subagent_ended hook" dist/subagent-registry-DLVHJynj.js
94:log$2.warn(`failed to emit subagent_ended hook for run ${runId}: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}`);

$ grep -n "failed to restore subagent runs" dist/subagent-registry-DLVHJynj.js
1911:log.warn(`failed to restore subagent runs from disk: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}`);
```

Line 94 is `emitSubagentEndedHookOnce` (the hook emission path). Line 1911 is `restoreSubagentRunsOnce` (the disk restore path). Before this fix, both were `catch { }` (empty) and `catch { // ignore restore failures }` respectively.

The `log$2` prefix on line 94 is the compiled form of the new `createSubsystemLogger("agents/subagent-registry-completion")`, while `log` on line 1911 is the existing `createSubsystemLogger("agents/subagent-registry")`.

**Live gateway startup proof**

The patched gateway starts, loads the subagent registry with both warning-enabled catch blocks, and runs cleanly:

```console
$ timeout 10 node openclaw.mjs gateway
2026-05-21T12:39:28.862-07:00 [gateway] loading configuration…
2026-05-21T12:39:28.917-07:00 [gateway] starting...
2026-05-21T12:39:33.527-07:00 [health-monitor] started (interval: 300s, startup-grace: 60s, channel-connect-grace: 120s)
2026-05-21T12:39:34.421-07:00 [gateway] agent model: openai/gpt-5.5 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
2026-05-21T12:39:34.422-07:00 [gateway] http server listening (8 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 5.5s)
2026-05-21T12:39:34.913-07:00 [gateway] ready
2026-05-21T12:39:52.803-07:00 [gateway] signal SIGTERM received
2026-05-21T12:39:52.807-07:00 [gateway] received SIGTERM; shutting down
2026-05-21T12:39:52.856-07:00 [shutdown] completed cleanly in 38ms
```

No `[agents/subagent-registry-completion]` or `[agents/subagent-registry]` warnings appear in the startup log, confirming the catch blocks are not triggered during normal operation (they are defensive logging for error paths only).

**Vitest test suites (supplemental)**

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry-completion.test.ts
 ✓ |agents-core| subagent-registry-completion.test.ts (8 tests) 86ms
 ✓ |agents-support| subagent-registry-completion.test.ts (8 tests) 73ms
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  16 passed (16)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts
 ✓ |agents-core| subagent-registry.test.ts (29 tests) 2679ms
 ✓ |agents-support| subagent-registry.test.ts (29 tests) 2264ms
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  58 passed (58)
```

**Fault injection proof: restore catch path fires in real gateway**

The `restoreSubagentRunsOnce` catch handler was triggered in a **live running gateway** by injecting `throw new Error("INJECTED: corrupt registry state")` at the top of the try block in the compiled `dist/subagent-registry-DLVHJynj.js`. This forces the catch path to fire during gateway startup.

**Steps:**
1. Back up the compiled subagent-registry bundle.
2. Inject `throw new Error(...)` inside the try block of `restoreSubagentRunsOnce` (after `restoreAttempted = true`, before `restoreSubagentRunsFromDisk`).
3. Start the gateway.
4. Observe the `log.warn` message in the gateway output.
5. Confirm the gateway continues to start normally (the catch swallows the error).
6. Restore the original bundle.

**Terminal output (fault injection):**

```console
2026-05-21T16:15:54.095-07:00 [gateway] starting...
2026-05-21T16:15:54.710-07:00 [agents/subagent-registry] failed to restore subagent runs from disk: INJECTED: corrupt registry state
2026-05-21T16:15:54.832-07:00 [gateway] starting HTTP server...
2026-05-21T16:15:56.662-07:00 [gateway] agent model: openai/gpt-5.5 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
2026-05-21T16:15:56.665-07:00 [gateway] http server listening (8 plugins; 2.6s)
2026-05-21T16:15:57.476-07:00 [gateway] ready
```

**Line-by-line analysis:**

- `[gateway] starting...`: Gateway startup begins.
- `[agents/subagent-registry] failed to restore subagent runs from disk: INJECTED: corrupt registry state`: The **`log.warn` in the catch handler fired**. The error message includes the full error text. Before this fix, this catch block was `catch { // ignore restore failures }` with no logging at all.
- `[gateway] ready`: The gateway **continued to start normally** despite the restore failure. The catch handler swallowed the error and let startup proceed.

Without this fix, the same error would be silently ignored (empty catch block with only a comment), leaving no diagnostic trail.

**Fault injection proof: hook emission catch path fires in real gateway**

The `emitSubagentEndedHookOnce` catch handler was also triggered in a **live running gateway**. A conditional `throw` was injected inside the try block of `emitSubagentEndedHookOnce` in `dist/subagent-registry-DLVHJynj.js` (line 73), and a timed trigger called the function after the gateway was ready.

**Terminal output (hook emission catch):**

```console
2026-05-21T21:35:44.608-07:00 [gateway] ready
2026-05-21T21:35:58.683-07:00 [agents/subagent-registry-completion] failed to emit subagent_ended hook for run INJECTED-PROOF-RUN: INJECTED: hook runner unavailable
2026-05-21T21:35:58.686-07:00 [gateway] signal SIGTERM received
2026-05-21T21:35:58.734-07:00 [gmail-watcher] gmail watcher stopped
2026-05-21T21:35:58.736-07:00 [shutdown] completed cleanly in 38ms
```

**Analysis:**
- `[agents/subagent-registry-completion]` subsystem prefix confirms the **new** `createSubsystemLogger("agents/subagent-registry-completion")` is active (this logger was added by this PR).
- `failed to emit subagent_ended hook for run INJECTED-PROOF-RUN: INJECTED: hook runner unavailable` is the exact `log.warn` message from the catch block at compiled line 94.
- The gateway continued running after the hook emission failure (the catch returns `false`, not rethrow).
- Before this fix, this catch block was `catch { return false; }` with no logging at all.

**Both catch blocks are now proven in live gateway runs:**
1. **Restore catch** (`restoreSubagentRunsOnce`): `[agents/subagent-registry] failed to restore subagent runs from disk: INJECTED: corrupt registry state`
2. **Hook emission catch** (`emitSubagentEndedHookOnce`): `[agents/subagent-registry-completion] failed to emit subagent_ended hook for run INJECTED-PROOF-RUN: INJECTED: hook runner unavailable`

Observed result after fix: Both new `log.warn` calls fire correctly in production compiled code. The restore catch logs under `[agents/subagent-registry]` and the hook emission catch logs under `[agents/subagent-registry-completion]`. Both allow the gateway to continue running. All 74 tests pass across both test files.

What was not tested: The hook emission was triggered via injected timer call rather than a real subagent completion event. The injection proves the catch handler mechanism (log.warn + return false + gateway continues) works identically to the restore path proof.

target.title: i18n(zh-CN): improve Chinese translations for Control UI
target.body: Improve Chinese (Simplified) translations across the Control UI, covering common UI strings, agent tabs, dreaming mode messages, cron job forms, and usage metrics. Replace ambiguous or overly technical translations with clearer alternatives. Key changes: - Replace "Dreaming" with "梦境" for all Dreaming-related strings - Improve jitter/stagger related translations for clarity - Fix untranslated strings in agent tabs, notes, and stats sections - Localize Tailscale serve references - Standardize zh-CN language display names - Update common section values for consistency ## Summary - Problem: Many Control UI strings remained in English or used awkward translations when viewed in zh-CN locale - Solution: Rewrote 30+ translations with clearer, more natural Chinese alternatives; added translations for previously untranslated strings - What changed: Updated `ui/src/i18n/locales/zh-CN.ts` - What did NOT change: All other locale files, non-locale source code, functionality or behavior ## Motivation Chinese-speaking users of the Control UI frequently encountered English strings or confusing translations (e.g. "Dreaming" left untranslated, "抖动" for stagger/jitter which has a different meaning in Chinese). This PR improves the overall zh-CN user experience by replacing ambiguous or overly technical translations with clearer alternatives more natural for native speakers. ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [x] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR N/A ## Real behavior proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Chinese (Simplified) locale had untranslated or awkward strings in the Control UI - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw gateway (2026.5.20) with zh-CN locale - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** 1. Modified zh-CN.ts with improved translations 2. Updated .i18n/zh-CN.tm.jsonl (translation memory) 3. Ran pnpm ui:i18n:sync to regenerate locale bundle 4. Loaded the updated gateway at server port 5. Set Control UI language to 简体中文 and hard-refreshed - **Evidence after fix:** - **Observed result after fix:** All targeted strings now display in improved Chinese (e.g., "梦境" instead of "Dreaming", "定时任务" instead of "Cron Jobs") - **What was not tested:** Visual rendering across all UI panels (spot-checked main sections) ## Root Cause N/A — not a bug fix. ## Regression Test Plan N/A — not a bug fix. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes zh-CN Control UI users will see: - "Dreaming" → "梦境" throughout dreaming section - "Cron Jobs" → "定时任务" in tabs, stats, and form fields - "Tailscale serve" → "Tailscale 服务" - "Browser enabled" → "浏览器已启用" - "Tool profile" → "工具配置" - Improved stagger/jitter terminology for clarity - Previously untranslated strings in restart confirmation dialog, empty job states, and job form now in Chinese ## Security Impact - New permissions/capabilities? **No** - Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No** - New/changed network calls? **No** - Command/tool execution surface changed? **No** - Data access scope changed? **No** ## Human Verification - Verified scenarios: Compared each changed string against the English source (en.ts) to ensure accuracy - Edge cases checked: Template interpolation patterns ({count}, {value}, {time}) preserved correctly; braces balanced; TypeScript structure intact - What I did **not** verify: Visual rendering (locale is compiled by Vite at build time) ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? **Yes** - Config/env changes? **No** - Migration needed? **No** ## Risks and Mitigations None — locale data change only, no logic, no API surface, no new dependencies. ## Real behavior proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Chinese (Simplified) locale had untranslated or awkward strings in the Control UI - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw gateway (2026.5.20) with zh-CN locale selected in Control UI settings - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** 1. Modified zh-CN.ts with improved translations 2. Updated .i18n/zh-CN.tm.jsonl (translation memory) 3. Ran pnpm ui:i18n:sync to regenerate locale bundle 4. Loaded the updated gateway at http://127.0.0.1:18789 5. Set Control UI language to 简体中文 and hard-refreshed - **Evidence after fix:** - **Observed result after fix:** All targeted strings now display in improved Chinese (e.g., \梦境\ instead of \Dreaming\, \定时任务\ instead of \Cron

commands.review_summary:
The PR exposes plugin approval action templates and keep-pending-without-route behavior across Gateway RPCs, plugin hooks/SDK rendering, approval view models, Web UI, native channel adapters, generated protocol models, docs, and focused tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is an additive plugin API and approval-surface feature, not a broken existing behavior report. Current-main inspection confirms the requested fields and explicit no-route pending mode are absent.

target.title: Expose plugin approval action metadata
target.body:
## Review context Concrete plugin: [Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit](https://github.com/Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit). The intent is to keep World ID / AgentKit out of OpenClaw core while letting external HITL plugins use the existing approval flow end-to-end. This PR's role: approval action metadata and no-route pending surface; it does not resolve verified approvals by itself. Bundle: - [#82431](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82431) exposes approval action metadata/UI rendering. - [#82434](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82434) adds verified approval resolution with plugin ownership checks. - [#82471](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82471) adds chat/status metadata and idempotency for durable approval updates. ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Agent participant Gateway participant Plugin as HITL plugin participant UI as TUI/Web UI participant Verifier as External verifier Agent->>Gateway: protected tool call Gateway->>Plugin: approval hook/request Gateway->>UI: approval card + plugin actions UI->>Plugin: user chooses verify once / trust session / deny Plugin->>Verifier: external proof flow Verifier-->>Plugin: verified result Plugin->>Gateway: resolve verified approval Gateway->>Agent: allow/deny blocked tool Plugin->>UI: status/update messages with idempotency ``` ```mermaid flowchart LR A["Approval action surface
#82431"] --> B["Verified resolver
#82434"] B --> C["Status/chat idempotency
#82471"] C --> D["Usable external HITL plugin flow"] ``` Trust boundary to review: should a plugin that already owns a pending approval be allowed to complete that specific approval after external verification, through a typed SDK/Gateway path with admin scope and ownership checks? Summary: - Add plugin approval action templates to the before_tool_call approval result and plugin.approval.request schema. - Expand templates with the server-generated approval id, store the resulting action commands, and carry them through reply payloads and approval view models. - Add keepPendingWithoutRoute so external HITL plugins can keep plugin approvals pending when no core approval route/client is active. - Keep native decision-button channel adapters compatible with command-only approval actions. - Update the generated Swift protocol model and plugin SDK API baseline for the intentional additive surface change. Verification: - `pnpm protocol:check` - `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` - `pnpm tsgo:extensions` - `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke` - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/discord/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts extensions/matrix/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/approval/handler-runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts src/infra/approval-view-model.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-reply.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-renderers.test.ts extensions/discord/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/matrix/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/approval/handler-runtime.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/approval/index.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.before-tool-call.test.ts` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts` - `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` ## Real behavior proof Behavior addressed: external plugins can attach approval-specific command actions to plugin approval requests and keep those requests pending while their own HITL command flow is responsible for resolution. Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, rebased onto current `origin/main` on May 16, 2026. Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the protocol generator/check, plugin SDK API baseline check, extension typecheck, plugin SDK strict declaration smoke, targeted extension oxlint, focused approval/gateway/plugin Vitest coverage, the host hook contract test, and whitespace diff check. Evidence after fix: ```text $ pnpm protocol:check wrote dist/protocol.schema.json wrote apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Sources/OpenClawProtocol/GatewayModels.swift $ pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256 $ pnpm tsgo:extensions (exit 0) $ pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke (exit 0) $ node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/discord/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts extensions/matrix/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/approval/handler-runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts Found 0 warnings and 0 errors. $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts src/infra/approval-view-model.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-reply.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-renderers.test.ts extensions/discord/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/matrix/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/approval/handler-runtime.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/approval/index.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.before-tool-call.test.ts Test Files 10 passed (10) Tests 122 passed (122) $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 50 passed (50) $ git diff --check origin/main...HEAD (no output) ``` Observed result after fix: the protocol and SDK baselines reflect the new additive action metadata, representative adapters preserve command actions, approval view/reply rendering carries those commands, and the before_tool_call host hook contract remains compatible. What was not tested: full release suite, live World ID callback flow, and the follow-up verified plugin-owned resolution API that is split into #82434. Refs #82336 ## External plugin integration proof Behavior addressed: the external ClawHub AgentKit plugin can consume the combined temporary OpenClaw host API branch that includes this PR's approval actions and no-route pending behavior. Real environment tested: GitHub Actions in `Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit` on May 16, 2026, checking out `Guardiola31337/openclaw@agentkit/external-plugin-host-apis` and linking it into the external plugin package. Exact steps or command run after this patch: the remote workflow checked out the temporary OpenClaw host branch, installed with frozen lockfiles, ran `pnpm build gatewayWatch`, ran `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke`, linked the host checkout into the AgentKit plugin, then ran `pnpm test:hitl` and `pnpm pack:check`. Evidence after fix: ```text $ gh run watch 25972624778 --repo Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit --exit-status ✓ main CI · 25972624778 ✓ build in 1m46s ✓ Run pnpm build gatewayWatch ✓ Run pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke ✓ Run pnpm dev:link-openclaw .openclaw-source ✓ Run pnpm test:hitl ✓ Run pnpm pack:check ``` Observed result after fix: the external plugin builds against the split OpenClaw host APIs, the plugin SDK declarations expose the needed approval/action surface, the AgentKit HITL proof passes, and the package dry-run includes the expected ClawHub plugin artifact. What was not tested: production World ID credential verification. This remote proof exercises the integrated temporary host branch because the external plugin requires this PR together with #82434 and #82471 for the full HITL path.

target.title: fix(slack): keep DM thread turns out of active steering
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve OpenClaw's stable Slack DM `SessionKey` contract for top-level DMs and ordinary DM thread replies.
- Track routed Slack thread ids on active reply operations, including agent-runner-owned, dispatch-owned, and queued follow-up operations.
- Suppress active-run steering only for Slack direct-message turns that carry Slack routed thread metadata and target a different Slack thread, so the fix no longer changes non-Slack IM steering behavior.

## Verification

- Confirmed latest `origin/main` at `1d1a7c26d8867fdfa98c97d8b510ad0ce1951858` still does not include this fix.
- Watched the original Slack regression fail before the fix: `expected true to be false` for `call.shouldSteer`, proving the second Slack DM thread would still steer into the active run.
- Watched the non-Slack compatibility regression fail before the Slack-only scoping change: `expected false to be true` for `call.shouldSteer`, proving the generic route-thread guard changed existing non-Slack same-session steering.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts -- -t "same-session Slack DM turns|non-Slack same-session turns"` - 1 file passed, 72 tests passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts -- -t "records routed Slack thread id|records queued thread id"` - 2 files passed, 205 tests passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.thread-session-key.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts` - 5 files passed, 372 tests passed.
- `git diff --check` - passed.
- Attempted `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run slack-dm-thread-isolation-check-changed --provider blacksmith-testbox --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`; it did not start because this local environment has no usable `crabbox` binary (`selected binary failed basic --version/--help sanity checks`).

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Two overlapping top-level Slack DMs from the same Slack sender should keep the stable DM `SessionKey`, but the second top-level Slack thread must not be steered into the first active run. Ordinary DM thread follow-up should continue to work in the original Slack thread. The steering guard is now Slack-scoped, so non-Slack same-session direct-message turns keep their prior steering behavior even when their transport thread ids differ.
- Real environment tested: Real Slack workspace `w1773415900-eqa641873.slack.com`, OpenClaw source checkout `323e3caa96b845ab9ac07a801c2ed2be06dd35f9`, foreground gateway started from this branch with `node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --port 18790 --verbose --ws-log compact`. The already-running installed gateway was `2026.5.12`, so I stopped it before the smoke and used the PR checkout gateway for the Slack event handling.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started the PR gateway with a temporary redacted proof config using Slack Socket Mode, `replyToMode=all`, and `allowBots=true` because the available automated Slack acceptance sender posts app-authored Slack messages. The OpenClaw bot under test was a different bot id, so this did not exercise self-message handling. Sent top-level DM A and top-level DM B from the same sender in the same DM channel 0.55s apart, then sent ordinary follow-up D inside B's Slack thread. After the Slack-only scoping change, ran the local regression set above to prove Slack `MessageThreadId`, Slack `TransportThreadId`, and non-Slack compatibility behavior.
- Evidence after fix: Redacted Slack API evidence from the live workspace:

```text
Gateway under test: OpenClaw 2026.5.24 (323e3ca)
Loaded channel runtime: Slack Socket Mode connected; octoclaw-runtime was not loaded in this proof gateway.
Workspace: w1773415900-eqa641873.slack.com
Sender: user=U...9Z bot_id=B...N2
OpenClaw bot: user=U...CQ bot_id=B...57
DM channel: D...QL
Nonce: oc-proof-mpjc9xn8

Top-level A sent: ts=1779600644.944599 thread_ts=1779600644.944599
Top-level B sent: ts=1779600645.499179 thread_ts=1779600645.499179
Delta between A and B sends: 0.55458s

A thread replies:
- user=U...9Z bot_id=B...N2 ts=1779600644.944599 thread_ts=1779600644.944599 text="[oc-proof-mpjc9xn8] top-level DM A... FINAL-A=oc-proof-mpjc9xn8-A"
- user=U...CQ bot_id=B...57 ts=1779600684.955289 thread_ts=1779600644.944599 text="... oc-proof-mpjc9xn8-A ..."

B thread replies:
- user=U...9Z bot_id=B...N2 ts=1779600645.499179 thread_ts=1779600645.499179 text="[oc-proof-mpjc9xn8] top-level DM B... FINAL-B=oc-proof-mpjc9xn8-B"
- user=U...CQ bot_id=B...57 ts=1779600690.273309 thread_ts=1779600645.499179 text="... FINAL-B=oc-proof-mpjc9xn8-B"

Ordinary DM thread follow-up in B:
- user=U...9Z bot_id=B...N2 ts=1779601018.905549 thread_ts=1779600645.499179 text="[oc-proof-mpjc9xn8] ordinary DM thread follow-up D in B thread... FINAL-D=oc-proof-mpjc9xn8-D"
- user=U...CQ bot_id=B...57 ts=1779601036.152919 thread_ts=1779600645.499179 text="Thread B continuation acknowledged ... FINAL-D=oc-proof-mpjc9xn8-D."

Local runtime transcript evidence:
- A trajectory contained stable SessionKey agent:main:slack:default:direct:u0...9z and wrote topic transcript ending in topic-1779600644.944599.jsonl.
- B trajectory contained the same stable SessionKey agent:main:slack:default:direct:u0...9z and wrote a separate topic transcript ending in topic-1779600645.499179.jsonl.
- B thread follow-up remained in Slack thread_ts=1779600645.499179 and received its own OpenClaw reply in that same thread.

Local compatibility proof after Slack-only scoping:
- Slack direct turn with MessageThreadId 501.000 vs active routeThreadId 500.000: shouldSteer=false, shouldFollowup=true.
- Slack direct turn with TransportThreadId 501.000 vs active routeThreadId 500.000: shouldSteer=false, shouldFollowup=true.
- Telegram direct turn with MessageThreadId 43 vs active routeThreadId 42: shouldSteer=true, shouldFollowup=true.
```

- Observed result after fix: The two overlapping top-level Slack DMs did not collapse into one Slack reply thread. A received an OpenClaw reply in A's Slack thread, B received an independent OpenClaw reply in B's Slack thread, and the later B-thread follow-up continued in B's thread. The local trajectories for A and B both carried the same stable DM `SessionKey` while using different routed Slack thread ids. The final active-run steering guard now applies only when `ChatType=direct`, routed thread metadata exists, and `Provider`, `Surface`, or `OriginatingChannel` resolves to `slack`.
- What was not tested: The automated sender available in this environment posts Slack app-authored messages, so the temporary proof config enabled `allowBots=true`. This live proof still exercises the real Slack Socket Mode transport, real Slack thread ids, and the PR checkout's active-run routing boundary; it does not prove Slack's human-user token UX separately. The local Crabbox/Testbox `pnpm check:changed` gate was attempted but could not start because the local `crabbox` binary is missing or unusable.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before the fix, the same local runtime scenario failed with `AssertionError: expected true to be false` at `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts:1306`, showing the same-session/different-thread second turn would steer into the active run. Before the Slack-only scoping update, the non-Slack compatibility test failed with `expected false to be true`, showing the generic guard would have changed non-Slack same-session steering.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Slack DMs intentionally share the stable direct-message session key, but the active-run steering decision originally compared only session identity. A second top-level DM from the same user could therefore steer into the active run for the first Slack message thread. A later generic route-thread guard fixed Slack but also changed other IM providers that may use same-session/different-thread steering, so the guard is now constrained to Slack direct-message routed-thread turns.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing Slack prepare tests covered the stable DM session contract, but auto-reply routing tests did not cover same-session, different-thread active runs, Slack `TransportThreadId` DM thread replies, non-Slack compatibility, or every operation creation path.
- Contributing context: The fix belongs at the active-run routing boundary, not in Slack session-key generation, because Slack DM session continuity is a shipped contract.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this: Unit test plus Slack prepare-path contract test.
- Target test or file: `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts`, and `extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.thread-session-key.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: Same stable Slack DM session, different routed Slack thread id, active prior run, second Slack turn must not steer into the first run; Slack DM thread replies using `TransportThreadId` get the same guard; non-Slack same-session direct-message turns remain steerable; every created active reply operation should carry the routed thread id when one exists.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It exercises the auto-reply decision that caused the overlap while separately preserving the Slack session-key contract, non-Slack compatibility, and the dispatch/follow-up operation creation paths ClawSweeper flagged.

target.title: fix(usage-cost): surface unpriced-model spend as missingCostEntries instead of a confident $0
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `openclaw gateway usage-cost` reports `totalCost: 0` with `missingCostEntries: 0` for models that ship an all-zero `cost` block (e.g. codex `gpt-5.5`, whose Codex backend exposes no per-token price). Real pay-per-token usage is therefore reported as a confident, *complete* `$0`, and every budget / spike / daily-spend safeguard that keys off `totalCost` is silently blind to it.
- **Solution:** In `scanTranscriptFile`, when a model's resolved pricing is *unknown* — no positive per-token rate and no tiered pricing — and the turn actually burned tokens, drop the transport's fabricated `$0` and surface the turn as a missing-cost entry instead of recording a confident `$0`.
- **What changed:** `src/infra/session-cost-usage.ts` — new `isModelPricingKnown()` helper and a sibling branch in `scanTranscriptFile` that mirrors the existing tiered-pricing override. `src/infra/session-cost-usage.test.ts` — regression test.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Models with positive or tiered pricing are unaffected; **operator-configured zero-cost models keep reporting a complete `$0`** (intentional free pricing is preserved); entries with zero token usage are not flagged; a transport-recorded *positive* cost is preserved; recorded costs for priced models are untouched. No change to model-catalog generation or the gateway pricing cache.

## Motivation

A freshly-onboarded, idle host on `2026.5.20` (default `openai/gpt-5.5`, `api_key` auth) accrued ~1.78M tokens/day, yet `usage-cost` reported `$0` with `missingCostEntries: 0`, so budget/spike monitoring never fired. Surfacing unknown-priced usage as *missing* lets downstream tooling tell "genuinely free" apart from "price unknown" instead of trusting a false `$0`. Tracks #85858.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #85858
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `usage-cost` reports a confident `$0` (`missingCostEntries: 0`) for unpriced `gpt-5.5` usage, hiding real spend from budget/spike monitoring.
- Real environment tested: self-hosted OpenClaw `2026.5.20 (e510042)` on Linux; default model `openai/gpt-5.5`; `api_key` auth.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: applied the equivalent change to the installed build, restarted the gateway, then ran `openclaw gateway usage-cost --days 2 --json --expect-final` over the same already-logged usage.
- Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output of the `openclaw gateway usage-cost` command — before vs. after the patch, same logs:

```
BEFORE (shipped build) -- openclaw gateway usage-cost --days 2 --json
  totals: { totalTokens: 1780235, totalCost: 0, missingCostEntries: 0 }

AFTER (patched build) -- same command, same already-logged usage
  totals: { totalTokens: 1780235, totalCost: 0, missingCostEntries: 32 }
```

- Observed result after fix: the same 1,780,235 tokens of otherwise-invisible unpriced usage are now surfaced as 32 missing-cost entries; `totalCost` stays `0` (no fabricated price is invented).
- What was not tested: models configured with *intentional* zero pricing (these would also be surfaced as missing — see scope note below); the macOS/Control-UI cost views.
- Before evidence: included in the terminal capture above.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause:** codex models ship `cost: {input:0, output:0, cacheRead:0, cacheWrite:0}` in the generated `models.json` because the Codex backend exposes no per-token price. `resolveModelCostConfig` returns that *defined* zero, `estimateUsageCost` computes a confident `0`, and the transport records `cost.total: 0` per turn. Cost aggregation (`applyCostTotal` / `applyCostBreakdown`) only treats `undefined` — not `0` — as missing, so "price unknown" is indistinguishable from "genuinely free."

## Note on the free-vs-unknown distinction

This fix is **source-aware** so it does not break intentionally-free pricing. A turn's all-zero cost is treated as *unknown* (counted toward `missingCostEntries`) only when **all** of these hold: the resolved pricing has no positive/tiered rate, the operator did **not** explicitly configure the model's price under `models.providers` (so the zero is a generated-catalog default, e.g. codex/gpt-5.x), and there is no trustworthy recorded cost (recorded cost is `0`/absent), and the turn actually burned tokens. A model the operator **deliberately** priced at `0`, or any turn carrying a real positive recorded cost, keeps reporting a complete `$0`. (Helper `resolveConfiguredModelCost()` reads config-only pricing to make this distinction; both paths have regression tests.)

Residual nuance for maintainers: this preserves *operator-configured* zero prices, but a non-codex provider whose models are emitted into the generated catalog with literal zeros would still be treated as unknown. Fully distinguishing that case wants an explicit pricing contract (e.g. `cost: null`/"unknown" vs `cost: 0`/"free") in the catalog schema — happy to follow whichever contract you prefer.

target.title: fix: add inbound peer send policy matching
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds native `session.sendPolicy.rules[].match.peerEquals: "inboundPeer"` support.
- Allows relational send-policy checks between the outbound target and the inbound peer for the current automatic-delivery turn, including `identityLinks` aliases.
- Threads inbound peer context through automatic replies, routed replies, tool context, plugin/gateway message-action context, and durable outbound delivery.
- Keeps send-policy-only predicates out of shared allow/deny scopes.
- Preserves explicit `message.send` compatibility by keeping explicit gateway/plugin/direct sends outside `session.sendPolicy` suppression.
- Reports blocked automatic durable sends as `denied_by_send_policy` suppression outcomes.
- Adds focused unit coverage and docs/help labels for the new predicate.

Fixes #85175.

## Verification

Latest head explicit-gateway-send follow-up validated locally: `fa5474b29b`.

Clean local worktree used: `~/projects/openclaw-pr-85763-send-policy`.

Commands run against latest head:

```text
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm exec vitest run src/sessions/send-policy.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts
pnpm exec vitest run src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts
node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/scripts/real-behavior-proof-policy.test.ts
```

Earlier broad validation on head `5b0f312431` before the schema-only follow-up:

```text
pnpm protocol:check
pnpm lint
pnpm check:test-types
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts
```

Observed local results on latest head:

```text
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile already up to date
```

target.title: refactor: internalize OpenClaw agent runtime
target.body:
## Summary

- rename the former Pi agent runtime/package surfaces to OpenClaw-owned agent runtime names
- internalize the runtime packages into core and wire the embedded OpenClaw runtime through the current plugin/runtime contracts
- remove copied session-export HTML assets, package-manager install/update leftovers, runtime `.pi` auth discovery, and stale internal Pi naming
- split modern agent runtime id helpers from deprecated Pi runtime-id compatibility; keep compatibility in explicit doctor/session/env/plugin-boundary helpers
- keep deprecated plugin SDK compatibility only at named boundaries, including `resolveLegacyAgentAccessToken`
- add the Pi third-party notice for incorporated code

## Verification

Behavior addressed: OpenClaw no longer has live core/runtime paths depending on Pi package names or `.pi` credential discovery; remaining Pi runtime-id compatibility is isolated in deprecated compat helpers for shipped config/session/env values and plugin SDK boundary compatibility.
Real environment tested: local macOS checkout plus Linux Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/aliases.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/backend.test.ts src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts src/agents/harness/runtime-plugin.test.ts src/agents/openai-codex-routing.test.ts src/agents/runtime-plan/build.test.ts src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -b tsconfig.projects.json`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs <changed files>`; `git diff --check`; `/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-scripts/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox ... corepack pnpm check:changed`.
Evidence after fix: targeted Vitest passed 13 files / 181 tests; local tsgo passed; targeted oxlint passed; `git diff --check` passed; autoreview clean; Testbox-through-Crabbox `tbx_01ksafhp87h8heqjsjwb7z9wgk` passed `pnpm check:changed` in Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26333664224.
Observed result after fix: `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/runtime.ts` is gone; modern runtime-id helpers live in `src/agents/agent-runtime-id.ts`; deprecated Pi alias handling is quarantined in `src/agents/deprecated-agent-runtime-compat.ts`; plugin SDK `resolveEmbeddedAgentRuntime` remains exported.
What was not tested: full Vitest suite and live provider usage calls.

target.title: fix(mcp): bound tools/list during catalog discovery
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the original hardening reused `connectionTimeoutMs` as the post-connect MCP `tools/list` request timeout, but that field is documented as connection-only and can hide slow-but-valid catalogs after upgrade.
- Solution: add an explicit optional `toolsListTimeoutMs` for post-connect MCP catalog listing, while preserving the previous SDK/default request-timeout behavior when it is not configured.
- What changed: config type/schema/help/labels/docs now expose `toolsListTimeoutMs`; MCP transport resolution threads it through; catalog discovery passes `{ timeout }` to `client.listTools(...)` only when the new field is set.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no MCP tool execution behavior, auth, transport selection, provider/tool inventory policy, pagination semantics, or default catalog request timeout behavior.

## Motivation

- Related #64411: MCP/plugin failures should degrade predictably instead of blocking the main path.
- Related #82842 covers startup/connect hardening; this PR covers the adjacent post-connect `tools/list` catalog phase without changing the connection-timeout contract.
- Related #63634 covers lenient MCP tool schema parsing; this PR does not change schema validation.
- This is intentionally a narrow compatibility-preserving availability hardening slice; #64411 remains broader follow-up scope.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #: N/A
- Related #64411
- Related #82842
- Related #63634
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: slow-but-valid bundled MCP catalogs remain visible by default even with a low `connectionTimeoutMs`, while an explicitly configured `toolsListTimeoutMs` can bound and degrade a hung post-connect `tools/list` request.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout at `d15d78d91b59919fb4368e2aa5cf7d599d24a28a` on Linux 6.17.0-14-generic x86_64, Node.js v22.22.1.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx /tmp/pr85063-slow-list-red-client.mjs
node --import tsx /tmp/pr85063-hanging-list-explicit-client.mjs
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts src/agents/mcp-transport-config.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/schema.test.ts src/config/schema.hints.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts
node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/cli/mcp.md docs/plugins/bundles.md
node --import tsx scripts/generate-base-config-schema.ts --check
node --import tsx scripts/generate-config-doc-baseline.ts --check
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
```

- Evidence after fix:

Terminal capture from this branch, copied live output:

```text
$ node --import tsx /tmp/pr85063-slow-list-red-client.mjs
{
  "elapsedMs": 558,
  "toolNames": [
    "slow_tool"
  ],
  "serverCount": 1,
  "serverLog": [
    "recv initialize",
    "send 0 response",
    "recv notifications/initialized",
    "delay tools/list 450",
    "recv tools/list",
    "send 1 response"
  ]
}

$ node --import tsx /tmp/pr85063-hanging-list-explicit-client.mjs
[bundle-mcp] failed to start server "hangingList" (/root/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin/node /tmp/pr85063-hanging-list-explicit-server.mjs): McpError: MCP error -32001: Request timed out
{
  "elapsedMs": 505,
  "toolNames": [],
  "serverCount": 0,
  "serverLog": [
    "recv initialize",
    "send 0 response",
    "recv notifications/initialized",
    "recv tools/list",
    "hang tools/list",
    "recv notifications/cancelled"
  ]
}
```

- Observed result after fix: without `toolsListTimeoutMs`, the slow 450 ms `tools/list` response produced `slow_tool` even though the fake server config used `connectionTimeoutMs: 250`; with `toolsListTimeoutMs: 250`, the hanging list request was cancelled and catalog discovery resolved degraded/empty in 505 ms.
- What was not tested: live third-party MCP servers, live SSE/streamable HTTP servers, malformed pagination/page-limit behavior, and full repository test suite. HTTP/SSE config threading is covered by resolver tests; source-runtime proof uses stdio.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before the compatibility fix, the same slow-list source-runtime repro with `connectionTimeoutMs: 250` and no `toolsListTimeoutMs` returned `toolNames: []`, `serverCount: 0`, and logged an SDK request timeout before the fake server's delayed `tools/list` response.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the earlier hardening applied the existing `connectionTimeoutMs` to `client.listTools(...)` after connection, broadening a documented connection-only field into a catalog-list request budget.
- Missing detection / guardrail: coverage had a hanging `tools/list` case, but not the compatibility case where a server connects quickly and returns a slow-but-valid catalog under the SDK default request timeout.
- Contributing context (if known): the existing catalog path already catches per-server listing failures and degrades; the missing piece was separating the post-connect list budget from the connection budget.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts`, `src/agents/mcp-transport-config.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: default catalog discovery calls `client.listTools(params)` without a timeout override, an explicit `toolsListTimeoutMs` bounds a hanging list request, and resolver output carries the new optional field for stdio and HTTP transports.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it uses the real session MCP runtime and stdio transport boundary for behavior, plus resolver tests for config threading, while keeping fake servers deterministic and local.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing startup/connect tests covered only connection failure/degrade behavior, not post-connect list compatibility.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A; this PR adds regression coverage.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Adds optional `mcp.servers.<name>.toolsListTimeoutMs` for MCP catalog listing. When omitted, OpenClaw preserves the SDK/default request-timeout behavior for `tools/list`; when set, a slow or hung post-connect catalog request is skipped/degraded after that timeout.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Default:
getCatalog() -> connectWithTimeout(connectionTimeoutMs) -> client.listTools(params) -> SDK/default request timeout remains in effect

Opt-in list timeout:
getCatalog() -> connectWithTimeout(connectionTimeoutMs) -> client.listTools(params, { timeout: toolsListTimeoutMs }) -> per-server failure is caught -> partial/empty catalog resolves
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No; this only bounds an existing catalog-listing request and does not execute MCP tools.
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux 6.17.0-14-generic x86_64
- Runtime/container: local source checkout, Node.js v22.22.1
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): bundled MCP stdio transport for source-runtime proof; resolver tests cover stdio and HTTP config threading
- Relevant config (redacted): fake slow-list MCP server with `connectionTimeoutMs: 250` and no `toolsListTimeoutMs`; fake hanging MCP server with `connectionTimeoutMs: 30000` and `toolsListTimeoutMs: 250`

### Steps

1. Run the two source-runtime smoke commands shown in `## Real behavior proof`.
2. Run the focused runtime/config regression tests:
   ```bash
   node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts src/agents/mcp-transport-config.test.ts
   ```
3. Run related schema/docs/type checks:
   ```bash
   node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/schema.test.ts src/config/schema.hints.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts
   node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/cli/mcp.md docs/plugins/bundles.md
   node --import tsx scripts/generate-base-config-schema.ts --check
   node --import tsx scripts/generate-config-doc-baseline.ts --check
   node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
   ```

### Expected

- A slow-but-valid `tools/list` response remains visible by default when no list timeout is configured.
- A hanging `tools/list` request degrades quickly when `toolsListTimeoutMs` is configured.
- `connectionTimeoutMs` remains a connection-only budget.

### Actual

- Slow-list source-runtime smoke resolved in 544 ms with `toolNames: ["slow_tool"]` and `serverCount: 1`.
- Explicit-timeout source-runtime smoke resolved in 379 ms with `toolNames: []`, `serverCount: 0`, and `notifications/cancelled` in the fake server log.
- Focused MCP/config tests passed: 4 files / 48 tests.
- Config/schema tests passed: 3 files / 76 tests.
- Docs format, generated config schema checks, generated config baseline check, and core test-project typecheck passed.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: corrected RED behavior before the fix, slow-but-valid default behavior after the fix, explicit list-timeout degradation after the fix, resolver threading for stdio and HTTP, schema/help/docs/generated-baseline alignment, and core test-project typecheck.
- Edge cases checked: non-positive `toolsListTimeoutMs` is ignored like an absent optional timeout, `connectionTimeoutMs` still defaults to 30 seconds when missing, low `connectionTimeoutMs` no longer shortens `tools/list` by default, and fake server cleanup/disposal paths complete.
- What you did **not** verify: live third-party MCP servers, live non-stdio transports, malformed pagination/page-limit behavior, and full repository test suite.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

The existing compatibility finding is addressed by this code update and proof; no manual bot conversation has been resolved in the GitHub UI.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes; this adds optional `mcp.servers.<name>.toolsListTimeoutMs`.
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A. Operators who want a shorter post-connect catalog-listing budget can opt in by setting `toolsListTimeoutMs` per MCP server.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a too-low `toolsListTimeoutMs` can still skip a slow-but-valid catalog.
  - Mitigation: the field is opt-in, and the default leaves the SDK/default request timeout in place for compatibility.
- Risk: hung catalogs still wait for the SDK/default request timeout unless the new field is configured.
  - Mitigation: this preserves existing behavior by default and gives operators an explicit list-timeout knob without changing the connection-timeout contract.
- Risk: generated config docs/baseline can drift when adding a new config field.
  - Mitigation: `generate-base-config-schema --check` and `generate-config-doc-baseline --check` passed after regenerating the baseline hash.

target.title: fix(auth): add --token flag to paste-token and surface validTypes in invalid_type warning
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #63042. Improves non-interactive auth setup and debuggability of auth profile load errors.

## Changes

- Adds `openclaw models auth paste-token --token <value>` for trusted scripted setup.
- Keeps the existing interactive `paste-token` prompt when `--token` is omitted.
- Rejects empty `--token` values before persisting credentials.
- Validates Anthropic setup-token values before persisting them.
- Includes accepted `validTypes` in invalid auth-profile warning logs.
- Documents `--token` and its argv exposure tradeoff in `docs/cli/models.md`.
- Adds an Unreleased changelog entry for the user-facing CLI fix.

## Security note

`--token <value>` is a convenience path for trusted shells and automation, but inline secrets can be exposed through shell history, command echoing, CI logs, or process-list tools. The CLI help and docs now say this explicitly and recommend the interactive prompt when possible.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: `models auth paste-token --token <value>` now runs through the real CLI without opening the interactive token prompt, updates the auth profile, and prints the normal config/profile update output.

Real environment tested: Local checkout of PR head `57874b9b64034f7b49719067b03f29683c909b33` on 2026-05-08, running the real OpenClaw CLI through `corepack pnpm openclaw` with an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```console
$ OPENCLAW_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw_pr63050_home \
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/private/tmp/openclaw_pr63050_home/.openclaw \
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/private/tmp/openclaw_pr63050_home/.openclaw/openclaw.json \
  corepack pnpm openclaw models auth paste-token \
    --provider openai \
    --profile-id proof:manual \
    --token proof-redacted-token
```

Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from the real CLI command above:

```console
Updated $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json
Auth profile: proof:manual (openai/token)
```

Observed result after fix: The command completed successfully without an interactive prompt and wrote the requested `proof:manual` OpenAI token auth profile in the isolated OpenClaw home.

What was not tested: A real provider token was not used; the changed behavior under test is CLI non-interactive token ingestion and profile persistence, not provider-side authentication.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/cli/models-cli.test.ts src/commands/models/auth.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.ensureauthprofilestore.test.ts`
- [x] `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/cli/models-cli.ts src/commands/models/auth.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/persisted.ts src/cli/models-cli.test.ts src/commands/models/auth.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.ensureauthprofilestore.test.ts docs/cli/models.md CHANGELOG.md`
- [x] `git diff --check`
- [x] Real CLI proof above shows `models auth paste-token --token` completes without an interactive prompt.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(cron): warn on main heartbeat handoff ghost runs
target.body:
Fixes #63106.

## Summary
- add a scoped warning for fast ok main-session systemEvent jobs using wakeMode=next-heartbeat
- annotate cron run logs with possible-main-next-heartbeat-ghost-run for that handoff path
- add cron.ghostRunWarningThresholdMs config with schema/help metadata and public docs

## Tests
- node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-cron.test.ts
- node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts src/config/zod-schema.cron-retention.test.ts src/config/schema.base.generated.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts src/cron/run-log.test.ts
- node --import tsx scripts/generate-base-config-schema.ts --check
- node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig tsconfig.oxlint.core.json src/gateway/server-cron.ts src/gateway/server-cron.test.ts src/config/types.cron.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts src/config/schema.help.ts src/config/schema.labels.ts src/cron/run-log.ts
- git diff --check

Latest PR-head verification also passed the full GitHub check set, with only neutral/skipped CodeQL remaining. Re-review retry note: the prior ClawSweeper run timed out before evaluating the latest proof/docs.

Note: local tsgo checks currently fail before this change on unrelated dependency/type issues (missing typebox and existing model compat errors).

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Verified that a fast successful main-session `systemEvent` cron handoff using `wakeMode: "next-heartbeat"` writes the `possible-main-next-heartbeat-ghost-run` warning into the cron run log.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS checkout on this PR head, using the real `buildGatewayCronService` and real cron run-log reader with an isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME`. This did not touch the real user cron store.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
$ OPENCLAW_HOME=$(mktemp -d /tmp/openclaw-ghost-proof.XXXXXX) node --import tsx -e '... buildGatewayCronService(...); add main next-heartbeat systemEvent job; force run; readCronRunLogEntries(...)'
```

- Evidence after fix: Redacted terminal output from the isolated run-log proof:

```json
{
  "home": "/tmp/openclaw-ghost-proof.redacted",
  "jobId": "redacted-job-id",
  "logPath": "/tmp/openclaw-ghost-proof.redacted/runs/redacted-job-id.jsonl",
  "latest": {
    "action": "finished",
    "status": "ok",
    "summary": "proof handoff",
    "runId": "cron:redacted-job-id:redacted-start-ms",
    "durationMs": 7,
    "deliveryStatus": "not-requested",
    "warnings": [
      "possible-main-next-heartbeat-ghost-run"
    ]
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The forced main-session `next-heartbeat` handoff completed successfully and the persisted run-log entry contains `warnings: ["possible-main-next-heartbeat-ghost-run"]`.
- Public docs proof: The branch now documents `cron.ghostRunWarningThresholdMs` in `docs/cli/cron.md` and `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`, including the default `50` ms threshold and `0` to disable.
- Additional gateway context: The same local machine also had a real running gateway reachable before this isolated proof (`openclaw gateway status` reported LaunchAgent loaded, runtime active, and connectivity probe ok).
- Focused regression test: `server-cron.test.ts > buildGatewayCronService > warns and annotates fast main next-heartbeat systemEvent handoffs`.
- What was not tested: I did not dispatch a real scheduled agent turn from the user's production cron store; the warning proof used an isolated temporary OpenClaw home with the real gateway cron service and run-log modules so it would not affect real scheduled jobs.

target.title: fix(auth): explain masked oauth profiles
target.body:
## Summary

- Normalize legacy `openai-codex` OAuth auth-profile aliases on load so older stored profiles remain usable.
- Add a targeted diagnostic when a stored auth profile exists for a provider but is skipped because `auth.profiles.<id>.mode` does not match the stored credential type.
- This now covers both #47964 failure modes: alias-shaped OAuth credentials and stale `mode=api_key` masking a valid OAuth profile.

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #47964
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause

There were two adjacent present-but-unusable OpenAI Codex OAuth paths:

- Older `auth-profiles.json` entries could contain legacy OAuth aliases such as `access_token`, `refreshToken`, `expires_at`, or `account_id`. The loader preserved those fields, but downstream PI credential conversion only reads canonical `access`, `refresh`, and finite `expires`, so the profile was ignored.
- A stale config override could set `auth.profiles.<id>.mode` to `api_key` while the stored credential was OAuth. Auth profile ordering skipped the stored OAuth credential, then the final error only reported a missing API key.

## What Changed

- Normalize known legacy OpenAI Codex OAuth aliases to canonical fields during persisted profile parsing.
- Keep canonical stored fields authoritative if both canonical and alias fields exist.
- Detect config/store auth-profile mode mismatches before throwing the final missing-auth error.
- Include a secret-free hint naming only profile/provider/mode metadata.
- Add regression coverage for both the legacy alias profile and the stale `api_key` mode conflict.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? Only compatibility normalization on read for known OpenAI Codex OAuth aliases
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

Diagnostics deliberately report only profile/provider/mode metadata. Regression coverage asserts OAuth access and refresh token values are not included in the diagnostic.

## Verification

- `corepack pnpm test src/agents/auth-profiles.ensureauthprofilestore.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm test src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts src/agents/pi-model-discovery.auth.test.ts extensions/openai/openai-codex-device-code.test.ts extensions/openai/openai-codex-provider.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/auth-profiles/persisted.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.ensureauthprofilestore.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Verified the auth-profile surface after this patch still exposes a real OpenAI Codex OAuth profile as an OAuth profile instead of masking it as missing API-key auth.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS OpenClaw install with a running LaunchAgent gateway on `127.0.0.1:18789`; real user auth-profile store under `~/.openclaw/agents/main/agent`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `openclaw gateway status`; `openclaw models auth list --json`.
- Evidence after fix: Redacted terminal output from the real setup:

```text
$ openclaw gateway status
Service: LaunchAgent (loaded)
Gateway: bind=custom (127.0.0.1), port=18789 (service args)
Runtime: running (pid redacted, state active)
Connectivity probe: ok
Capability: admin-capable

$ openclaw models auth list --json
{
  "agentId": "main",
  "provider": null,
  "profiles": [
    {
      "id": "openai-codex:default",
      "provider": "openai-codex",
      "type": "oauth",
      "label": "openai-codex:default",
      "expiresAt": "2026-05-02T08:28:49.000Z"
    }
  ]
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The real gateway was reachable and the auth listing completed without exposing secrets; the OpenAI Codex profile was surfaced as `type: "oauth"` in the live profile list rather than being treated as an API-key-only missing-auth case.
- What was not tested: I did not perform a fresh OpenAI Codex OAuth browser/device-code login during this verification. The focused regression tests cover legacy alias normalization and stale mode mismatch diagnostics.
